title: Function, member, lambda, IIFE, and defer grammar
summary: Functions read with def and trailing returns, member receiver mutability is explicit, lambdas read as nested functions, and immediately invoked lambdas remain distinct result-producing constructs.
details: A function declaration's leading auto becomes green def; C-style leading return types are moved to a trailing arrow on the row containing the closing parenthesis, including multiline C declarations, without changing source rows.
Parameters move to name: type and follow BHV-003; default arguments remain visible.
A non-const non-static member gains mut after its parameter list; const and static members do not.
A mutable lvalue-reference return gains a mut marker before its type; a read-only reference return retains explicit const under the current return-type rule.
A lambda assigned to auto becomes lambda name(captures | parameters); by-reference capture ampersands move to name suffixes and the separator appears only when captures exist.

A multiline IIFE assigned to a binding is rendered as a result declaration using its explicit return type, while the lambda body and invocation rows remain distinct; ambiguous single-line/deduced-return IIFEs remain raw.

DANS_DEFER and DEFER lambda wrappers become defer statements or blocks: a one-statement body is shown directly after defer, while a multi-statement body keeps braces and one displayed row for every source row.
Copy/move constructor and assignment ceremony can collapse to $copy, $move, $copya, and $movea; default constructors and destructors remain named.

provenance.note: Function signature movement, member markers, parameter chunks, lambdas, IIFEs, and defer are implemented here.
Copy/move signature collapsing is a focused raw-line renderer.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_spec.lua lambda, defer, function, and special-member cases
evidence.claim: Supported and deliberately raw function-shaped forms are pinned.
Parameter colors and integrated function/member spellings agree.

questions: How are lambdas visually separated from IIFEs?
Where is member-function receiver mutability displayed?
Why do default constructors remain named while copy constructors collapse?

last_verified.method: Function/lambda render branches, special-member module, C ABI multiline cases, focused cases, and header goldens reviewed.
